articles.name: How can I reconnect Zendesk QA?
articles.title: How can I reconnect Zendesk QA?
articles.body:
Question

Some of my tickets do not import into Zendesk QA. What can I do?

Answer

If tickets do not appear in Zendesk QA, the connection to your help desk likely expired. This usually happens after a password change, an admin change, or a bulk edit in your help desk. Zendesk QA pauses the import of new conversations until you renew the connection.

To access Connections:

  1. Select the Settings icon (your profile photo)
  2. Select the Toggle sidebar icon
  3. Under Account, select Connections
  4. Check whether the connection status is Active or Inactive
  5. If the connection is inactive, select the icon with three dots on the right, then select Renew connection
Note: The Renew connection button is only available for Zendesk QA accounts that did not migrate from Klaus.
 
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why do some tickets not import into Zendesk QA? Why do tickets stop appearing or sync pause? Why are new conversations missing in my QA tool?

Tickets do not import when the connection to your help desk or ticketing system expires. This connection drop usually happens after a password change, an admin or support person change, or a bulk edit in your help desk. Zendesk QA pauses the import of new conversations automatically until you renew the connection.

How do I reconnect Zendesk QA? How do I renew my help desk connection? How do I fix an inactive connection?

You can restore your connection through the settings menu. Follow these steps to reconnect:

  1. Select the Settings icon, which displays as your profile photo.
  2. Select the Toggle sidebar icon.
  3. Under Account, select Connections.
  4. Check whether the connection status displays as Active or Inactive.
  5. If the connection status is inactive, select the icon with three dots on the right side, then select Renew connection.

Can I renew my connection if my account migrated from Klaus? Is the Renew connection button available for migrated accounts?

No. You can only use the Renew connection button if your Zendesk QA account did not migrate from Klaus.

articles.name: Why can't I see the full bot conversation at the start of a messaging ticket?
articles.title: Why can't I see the full bot conversation at the start of a messaging ticket?
articles.body:
Question

When a new messaging ticket arrives, I don't see the full bot conversation the user went through. Why doesn't the ticket include all the bot interactions?

Answer

You can see the initial bot interaction for 72 hours after the user's last message. After 72 hours without activity, the system clears that interaction and restarts it when the user returns to the bot.

If a user follows the flow and stays inactive for more than 72 hours, the next user message resets the conversation and resumes from where the user left off in the flow. If that user transfers to an agent, the system creates a new messaging ticket, and you do not see the earlier bot interaction history from before that inactivity period.

If a user starts a bot conversation and remains active in the Web Widget and sends messages within 72 hours, you see the full conversation history in the ticket.

Tip: When a messaging conversation approaches inactivity, Zendesk can send inactivity reminders to the user. Admins can now add tags to those reminder tickets to help track inactive conversations, identify follow-ups, and build reporting workflows around them. For more information, see this article: Sending conversation inactivity reminders to end users

For an entire list of messaging resources, see Web and mobile messaging resources.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why can't I see the full chatbot conversation history at the start of a messaging ticket? Why does a messaging ticket clear the AI agent interaction history? Why is the chat history missing when an end user transfers to a team member?

You only see the initial chatbot conversation history for 72 hours after the customer sends their last message. After 72 hours of inactivity, the system clears the interaction and resets the history when the customer returns to the AI agent.

Can I see the full chat history if the customer remains active? Does Zendesk preserve the chatbot interaction history when messages occur within 72 hours? Will the messaging ticket show the entire conversation if the end user stays active?

Yes. If the customer starts a chatbot conversation, remains active in the Web Widget, and sends messages within 72 hours, you see the full conversation history in the messaging ticket.

What are the time limits for viewing AI agent conversations in a messaging ticket? What happens to the chat history after 72 hours of inactivity? When does Zendesk reset the conversation history and create a new messaging ticket?

The system enforces a 72-hour inactivity limit. If a customer remains inactive for more than 72 hours and then resumes the flow, the next message resets the conversation. If that customer transfers to a team member, the system creates a new messaging ticket and removes the earlier chatbot interaction history.

How can I track inactive messaging conversations? Can admins add tags to conversation inactivity reminders? How do I build reporting workflows for inactive tickets?

Admins can add tags to conversation inactivity reminders sent to customers. Use these tags to track inactive conversations, identify follow-ups, and build reporting workflows.

articles.name: How does Zendesk send email notifications?
articles.title: How does Zendesk send email notifications?
articles.body:
Question

How do I send an email? Are emails sent as soon as I submit a ticket?

Answer

Before Zendesk sends an email notification, you must meet the delivery prerequisites. You must have at least one verified email address. You must verify DNS records. The recipient must have an active email address.

Email notifications use a multi-layered system. Business rules, such as triggers and automations, determine when notifications are sent.

Triggers fire immediately when conditions are met. Triggers execute in real time.

By default, the system includes triggers to send notifications to customers for these events:

  • When their new request is received
  • When an agent submits a public comment

The default triggers also notify agents of these events:

  • When a new request is received
  • When the agent is assigned a ticket
  • When a request is assigned to the group of an agent
  • When an end user submits a comment to an assigned ticket

Automations can also notify customers. Apart from the Request customer satisfaction rating (system automation), no other default automation sends emails to customers. Automations run once per hour and check time-based conditions.

Each trigger or automation references an email template:

  • Templates define content and appearance
  • Templates use placeholders: {{ticket.title}}, {{ticket.description}}
  • You can customize templates in Admin Center > Channels > Email > Templates

Alternatively, if the plan has access to Advanced AI Agents, an AI agent for email can configure a generative reply in an email from a messaging conversation instead. For more information, see About the Generative replies block in conversation flows for advanced AI agents.

To check which which action sent the notification email on a ticket based on your setup, check the Events view of the ticket for any Email notification event.

Note: Email conversation flows are available by default for any new email in advanced AI agents.
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

What is the email notification system? How does Zendesk send email notifications? What determines when notification rules or auto-replies go out?

Email notifications use a multi-layered system. Business rules, such as triggers or auto-replies and automations or time-based rules, determine when notifications go out.

Can I send an email notification immediately? Are emails sent as soon as I submit a ticket? Do triggers or auto-replies execute in real time?

Yes. Triggers or auto-replies fire immediately when conditions are met. These rules execute in real time.

What are the prerequisites for email delivery? What limits or conditions apply before Zendesk sends an email notification? What do I need to verify to send notifications to a customer or end user?

You must meet specific delivery prerequisites before the system sends a notification. You must have at least one verified email address and you must verify DNS records. Additionally, the recipient or customer must have an active email address.

How do I customize templates? Where do I edit email templates in the Admin Center?

You can customize the content and appearance of templates by navigating to Admin Center Channels Email Templates.

Can I use AI agents or chatbots to reply to emails? How do advanced AI agents configure generative replies?

Yes. If your plan type or pricing tier has access to Advanced AI Agents or chatbots, an AI agent for email can configure a generative reply in an email from a messaging conversation instead. Email conversation flows are available by default for any new email in advanced AI agents.

How do I check which action sent the notification email on a ticket? How do I find the email notification event?

You can check the specific action that sent the notification email by performing the following steps:

  1. Open the ticket.
  2. Open the Events view of the ticket.
  3. Look for any Email notification event.

articles.name: How do I export a list of users?
articles.title: How do I export a list of users?
articles.body:
Question

I want to get a list of users in Zendesk Support. How can I download my user information to share outside of my account?

Answer

Use basic data export

Basic data export functionality is available to these Support and Suite plans. An account admin or custom role with export permissions can export a list of users in the XML or JSON format. Agents can only export users with the API. Download the file and manually filter it with software like Excel or Google Sheets.

For Enterprise accounts, admins can use a granular permission to restrict CSV exports. If you use a custom role and cannot see the export options, ensure your admin has enabled the CSV Export permission for your role.

Verify your plan against these plan requirements. If your plan allows exports, yet you do not see the Reports option, contact Zendesk Customer Support to request the feature.

Use an add-on

Accounts that purchased the customer list add-on can export user lists in a CSV file. This feature targets groups of users and includes the information of your choice in an export. Use the customer list add-on to export enhanced user list views, customer segmentation, or to schedule exports.

  • Enterprise Plans: Exporting CSV files from customer lists requires a custom role with the CSV Export permission enabled
  • Contributor Role: The Contributor role is a viewer-only role and does not support CSV exports. To allow an agent to export lists, they must be assigned to a Light Agent role or a custom role with export permissions.

Use the API

The REST API Users endpoint is available to all Zendesk accounts. The API gives you flexibility and control, but requires the assistance of someone with basic knowledge of code. If you have developer resources on your team, the API is a good option.

Use the Marketplace

Explore third-party alternatives to install from the Zendesk Marketplace, for example, the Advanced Search Plus app. In that app in Support, use the * search parameter to return all Users.

This article is provided for instructional purposes only. Zendesk cannot provide support for third-party apps. Reach out to the email address of the developer under App details, from the app page in the Marketplace, or search for a solution online.
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

How do I export a list of users? How can I download my user information or customer data to share outside of my account? How do I extract my assignee or end user records?

You can export a list of users by using the basic data export functionality, purchasing the customer list add-on, utilizing the REST API, or installing a third-party alternative app from the Zendesk Marketplace.

Can I download user data as a CSV file? Can agents export user lists? Can a read-only agent export customer information?

Accounts with the customer list add-on can export user lists in a CSV file. Agents can only export users with the API. The Contributor role is a viewer-only role and does not support CSV exports; you must assign agents to a Light Agent role or a custom role with export permissions to allow exports.

What formats does the basic data export support? What permissions do I need to export data? Why can I not see the data export options?

The basic data export supports XML or JSON formats. An account admin or a custom role with export permissions can export the list. For Enterprise accounts, ensure your admin enables the CSV Export permission for your custom role. If your plan allows exports but you do not see the Reports option, contact Zendesk Customer Support.

What is the customer list add-on? Why should I use the customer list add-on or paid plugin?

The customer list add-on is a feature that targets groups of users and includes the information of your choice in a CSV export. Use this add-on to export enhanced user list views, perform customer segmentation, or schedule exports.

How do I use the API to export users? How do I use the Zendesk Marketplace to download customer data?

  1. Access the REST API Users endpoint, which is available to all Zendesk accounts and requires basic knowledge of code.
  2. Explore third-party alternatives to install from the Zendesk Marketplace, such as the Advanced Search Plus app.
  3. Use the * search parameter in the app within Support to return all Users.

articles.name: What counts as an agent license in Zendesk?
articles.title: What counts as an agent license in Zendesk?
articles.body:
Question

How can I determine who uses an agent seat in my account?

Answer

These roles use an agent seat:

  • Any agent, the account owner, and an admin use a seat
  • Suspended agents use a seat
  • Light agents and contributors are usually free, but they use a seat when their role appears as Agent, Admin, or Editor for Chat, Knowledge, or Analytics
  • Additional roles require a Support seat. The requirement depends on the product.
Note: A Sell account owner uses a seat in both Sell and Suite plans.

To free up agent seats, deselect these sub-roles and products or downgrade agents to end users.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

What is an agent license? What counts as an agent seat in Zendesk? Who uses an agent count?

An agent license or subscription determines your available staff capacity. Any agent, the account owner, and an admin use a seat. Suspended agents also use a seat. A Sell account owner uses a seat in both Sell and Suite plans. Additional roles require a Support or ticketing system seat depending on the product.

Who is a light agent or read-only agent? Do contributors or viewers use an agent seat? Can a light agent take up an agent count?

Light agents or read-only agents and contributors are usually free. However, they use a seat when their role appears as Agent, Admin, or Editor for Chat, Knowledge, or Explore or analytics.

How do I free up agent seats? How can I reduce my agent count or subscription? How do I release an agent license?

  1. Deselect these sub-roles and products.
  2. Downgrade agents to end users or customers.

articles.name: How do I reduce the number of agent seats on my subscription?
articles.title: How do I reduce the number of agent seats on my subscription?
articles.body:
Reducing the number of agent seats

If you have an unused agent seat on your account, you can remove it from your subscription. You must be the account owner to make these changes. To view the number of active seats on your account, visit the Team members page.

Warning: Before you remove agent seats from your account, ensure the seats are empty so you do not lose tickets or other data. For more information, see Best practices for removing agents.

Customers with Zendesk sales-assisted or managed accounts may need to contact their account representative to make this change.

To make this change as a self-service customer:

  1. Follow the instructions to purchase agent seats
  2. Update the number of agent seats you need in the Seats field. You can reduce or add seats. 

These changes won't go into effect until your next billing cycle. For more information, see Why did my subscription change not go into effect immediately?

articles.name: Can I edit closed tickets?
articles.title: Can I edit closed tickets?
articles.body:
Question

Can I edit a closed ticket to add information?

Answer

Admins can edit the tags, subject, and priority ticket fields on closed tickets when the modify closed tickets feature is active in the account. To do it, use Agent Workspace or the Update Ticket and Update Many Tickets endpoints.

Zendesk lets you edit tickets in solved status before they are closed through business rules. Requestors can only create follow-up tickets from closed tickets. Business rules such as triggers and automations don't act on closed tickets. For more information, see About system ticket rules. 

You can delete closed tickets but you can't mass delete them from a ticket view or a user profile. For more information, see How can I bulk delete closed tickets?

Note: Closed, merged and archived tickets show the organization update in their ticket events.

If your account uses the Agent Workspace, you can redact ticket content redact closed tickets.

Can I edit tickets in solved status before they close? Can you change solved tickets?

articles.name: How can I add additional ticket type options?
articles.title: How can I add additional ticket type options?
articles.body:
Question

Can I add additional ticket type options?

Answer

The default type field can't be modified because it is a system field. There are four values for ticket type:

  • Question
  • Incident
  • Problem
  • Task

Because it's not a required ticket field, you can leave it blank.

If you prefer, you can create your own custom dropdown field with more values for ticket type. You can then use this custom ticket field instead of the native ticket field.

 To implement this solution

  1. In Admin Center, click Objects and rules in the sidebar, then select Tickets > Fields.
  2. Hover over the Type field, then click the options menu icon () and select Deactivate. For information on the consequences of deactivating a ticket field, see this article: Understanding how creating, deactivating, or deleting ticket fields impacts tickets.
  3. Add a new field and select Drop-down as the field type.
  4. Enter a name under Display name.
  5. Under Field values, add the custom values for your own type field.
  6. Select Save.

For a guide on creating custom ticket fields, see the article: Adding custom fields to your tickets and support request form.

Note: If you deactivate the system Type field, all tickets default to the Incident type. A custom label by ticket ID can't be added at the top of the page and the system can't leave that space blank.
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Can I add additional ticket type options? Can you customize native ticket types? Can I add custom options to the default type field?

You cannot modify or add options to the default ticket type field because it is a fixed system field. The system only provides four native options: Question, Incident, Problem, and Task. If you need more options, you can create a new custom drop-down field with your preferred choices and use it instead of the native field.

How do I add custom values for ticket types? How do I create a custom dropdown field for ticket type? How do I change the options for ticket type?

You can replace the native type field with a custom field by following these steps:

  1. Navigate to the Admin Center, click Objects and rules in the sidebar, and select Tickets > Fields.
  2. Hover over the Type field, click the options menu icon (three vertical dots), and select Deactivate.
  3. Click to add a new field and select Drop-down as the field type.
  4. Enter a name under Display name.
  5. Add your custom categories under Field values.
  6. Select Save.

What happens if I deactivate the system Type field? What are the limitations of deactivating the native Type field?

When you deactivate the system Type field, all tickets default to the Incident type automatically. Additionally, you cannot add a custom label by ticket ID at the top of the page, and the system cannot leave that specific area blank.

articles.name: How can I reconnect my WhatsApp number in Zendesk?
articles.title: How can I reconnect my WhatsApp number in Zendesk?
articles.body:
Question

To apply for a name change for my WhatsApp channel, I need to disconnect and reconnect my WhatsApp number. How can I reconnect my WhatsApp number in Zendesk?

Answer

Warning: Before you proceed with these instructions, ensure you have access to your Meta Suite account and you can receive voice calls or SMS on the phone tied to the number you try to reconnect.
  1. Deactivate the 2 factor authentication for your number.
  2. Delete your Whatsapp channel. In Admin center > Channels > Messaging and social > Messaging click the three dots icon of the channel you want to delete and click Delete.

     
  3. Navigate to your Meta Suite account and select your Whatsapp Business Account. Then, click the Whatsapp Manager button.


     
  4. From your WhatsApp Manager dashboard, click the number you want to reconnect, navigate to the profile tab and check if a registration is required 

  5. If needed, proceed with the registration. If there is no prompt, you can ignore this step.
  6. Return to Zendesk and add your Whatsapp channel again.
Note: If the reconnection throws an error, don't try to reconnect the channel again. This could lock your Whatsapp number for 72 hours. Instead, contact Zendesk customer support.
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

How do I reconnect my WhatsApp number in Zendesk? How can I reconnect my WhatsApp number in my account or instance? How do I change my WhatsApp channel name?

To reconnect your WhatsApp number or change your channel name in your Zendesk account or instance, follow these steps:

  1. Deactivate the two-factor authentication for your number.
  2. Delete your WhatsApp channel. In Admin center Channels Messaging and social Messaging, click the three dots icon of the channel you want to delete and click Delete.
  3. Navigate to your Meta Suite account, select your WhatsApp Business Account, and click the WhatsApp Manager button.
  4. Click the number you want to reconnect from your WhatsApp Manager dashboard, navigate to the profile tab, and check if a registration is required.
  5. Proceed with the registration if needed. If there is no prompt, you can ignore this step.
  6. Return to Zendesk and add your WhatsApp channel again.

What do I need before I reconnect my WhatsApp number? What are the prerequisites to reconnect my WhatsApp number?

Before you proceed with the instructions, ensure you have access to your Meta Suite account. Ensure you can receive voice calls or SMS on the phone tied to the number you try to reconnect.

What happens if my WhatsApp reconnection throws an error? Can a failed WhatsApp reconnection lock my number? What should I do if my WhatsApp channel reconnection fails?

Do not try to reconnect the channel if the reconnection throws an error. This action can lock your WhatsApp number for 72 hours. Contact Zendesk customer support or help desk.

articles.name: How do I verify forwarding, SPF, DNS, and TXT records in Zendesk?
articles.title: How do I verify forwarding, SPF, DNS, and TXT records in Zendesk?
articles.body:
Question

How can I check my SPF records? Where do I confirm if my DNS records are correct? How can I see if TXT records are configured?

Answer

Important: Wait up to 48 hours for the check marks in the email settings to turn green. The email admin configures these records outside Zendesk, and they take time to update in the account.

Only an admin can verify a successful DNS setup with these options:

  • Verify in Zendesk
  • Verify outside of Zendesk
Note: Zendesk auto-verifies the TXT record when you add a support address. Zendesk may require you to add the value to the DNS record in the future. For more information, see Allowing Zendesk to send email on behalf of your email domain.

Verify in Zendesk

To verify in Zendesk:

  1. Go to Email settings in Admin Center
  2. Click Manage support addresses
  3. Look for the Failed verification status, which indicates an email verification error
  4. Click the three dots, then select Verify forwarding, Verify SPF, or Verify DNS as needed
  5. Click Verify to start the check
  6. Wait up to 48 hours for the records to update. Check the external inbox and Sent folder, and the tickets to confirm that emails are sent and received.
Note: Each email setup is different, and some DNS records are optional. The configuration may not require you to fix all three errors.

To help prevent this error:

  • Check the support addresses in the email settings on a regular cadence
  • Ask the email admin to share any external changes that can affect email forwarding to Zendesk

Verify outside of Zendesk

Email forwarding verification

To verify the custom email address, Zendesk sends a verification email to the email server. The email server must automatically return that verification to the Zendesk account.

If Zendesk creates emails by forwarding but the forwarding status shows Failed, ask the email provider to confirm that the outbound email server doesn’t block the verification email.

SPF and DNS verification

After you fix the DNS records with the email admin, or if you still have send issues, verify the records outside Zendesk to detect any problems.

Many email admins use third-party tools such as MXtoolbox.com or DMARC Analyzer to diagnose issues with DNS records. The example below uses MXtoolbox.com.

Note: Resolving these errors requires steps outside the Zendesk account. Contact Zendesk Customer Support for further help. This article shows example steps in third-party tools, which may differ from how the email admin resolves the error.

To verify outside of Zendesk:

  1. Open the third-party tool you use to troubleshoot
  2. Go to the section for the DNS record test: SPF Record Lookup, DNS Check, and TXT Lookup
  3. Enter the record in the field, format it correctly, and press Enter to start the lookup. The test steps differ slightly by record.
    • To test an SPF record, copy the text after the @ sign in the company email, the domain.com. For example, copy myexamplecompany.com
    • To test a DNS record, enter the email domain. For example, myexamplecompany.com
    • To test the TXT record, add zendeskverification in front of the email domain. For example, enter zendeskverification.myexamplecompany.com
  4. The tool shows a green verification message for the record. See the examples below:
    • This SPF record includes the recommended information and shows a green Pass
    • This DNS check shows green verification check marks and no glaring issues
    • The TXT record is found and passes verification
  5. If the record doesn’t pass the external verification check, the error message from the third-party tool helps the email admin identify what to fix in the DNS record
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

How do I verify my DNS records? Who can check the SPF setup? What access level do I need to confirm DNS records?

Only an admin or a user with the correct permissions or access level can verify a successful DNS setup. You use verification options inside your help desk or ticketing system, or you check the records using tools outside of your account or instance.

Does Zendesk automatically check TXT records? How do TXT records get verified when I add a support address? What is the automatic verification process for TXT records?

The ticketing system automatically verifies the TXT record when you add a support address. The system may require you to add the value to the DNS record in the future.

How do I verify email records in Zendesk? Where do I check for a "Failed verification" status? How do I start the record check in my instance?

To verify records inside your help desk or ticketing system, follow these steps:

  1. Go to Email settings in Admin Center.
  2. Click Manage support addresses.
  3. Look for the "Failed verification" status, which indicates an email verification error.
  4. Click the three dots, then select Verify forwarding, Verify SPF, or Verify DNS as needed.
  5. Click Verify to start the check.
  6. Wait up to 48 hours for the records to update, and check the external inbox, Sent folder, and tickets to confirm that emails send and receive.

How long do DNS updates take? Do I need to fix every email error? How long must I wait for the email check marks to turn green?

You wait up to 48 hours for the check marks in the email settings to turn green. The email admin configures these records outside the ticketing system, and they take time to update in the account or instance. Each email setup is different, some DNS records are optional, and the configuration may not require you to fix all three errors.

Why does my email forwarding status show "Failed"? What do I do if the outbound email server blocks the verification email? How does the system verify a custom email address?

To verify the custom email address, the ticketing system sends a verification email to the email server, and the email server must automatically return that verification to the account or instance. If the forwarding status shows "Failed", ask the email provider to confirm that the outbound email server does not block the verification email.

How do I verify SPF and DNS records outside Zendesk? What external tools check my email records? How do I perform a lookup for TXT records externally?

Many email admins use third-party tools such as MXtoolbox.com or DMARC Analyzer to diagnose issues with DNS records. To verify records outside the ticketing system, follow these steps:

  1. Open the third-party tool you use to troubleshoot.
  2. Go to the section for the DNS record test, such as SPF Record Lookup, DNS Check, or TXT Lookup.
  3. Enter the record in the field, format it correctly, and press Enter to start the lookup. To test an SPF record, copy the text after the @ sign in the company email (the domain.com). To test a DNS record, enter the email domain. To test the TXT record, add zendeskverification in front of the email domain.
  4. Confirm the tool shows a green verification message, such as a green Pass for SPF, green check marks for DNS, or a found TXT record.
  5. If the record does not pass the external check, use the error message to help the email admin identify what to fix in the DNS record. External resolutions occur outside the ticketing system, and you can contact Zendesk Customer Support for further help.

How do I prevent email verification errors? What are the best practices to maintain support addresses? How can I avoid forwarding errors in my ticketing system?

To help prevent these errors, check the support addresses in the email settings on a regular cadence. Ask the email admin to share any external changes that can affect email forwarding to your help desk or ticketing system.

articles.name: How can I tell if Zendesk is sending email notifications to customers?
articles.title: How can I tell if Zendesk is sending email notifications to customers?
articles.body:
Confirm if Zendesk sends email notifications to customers

  1. Activate your triggers. The standard support triggers that begin with Notify requester of... send communication to end users. These triggers must be active to reach end users. See Customers don't receive emails. If you customize triggers, check that any trigger meant to send notifications to end users has conditions that tickets meet. Ensure that your triggers contain the correct placeholder to pull in ticket data.

  2. Check a recent ticket. View the events of your ticket. Look for a recent update from an agent. The Email notification label in the audit trail represents the email notifications, followed by an ID number. Click the ID next to a notification to view the email notification.

    If the Email notification event doesn't exist in the event log, an email notification wasn't sent.

  3. In accounts with the Zendesk Agent Workspace activated, you can also view reasons for email delivery failure directly from the comment.

    If you see a delivery failure message, see Understand email delivery failures in the Agent Workspace.

Ask end users to check their spam or junk folders for email notifications.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

How do I know if an email notification went out from a ticket? How do I view the email notification audit trail in my help desk? Can I check the event log to confirm Zendesk sent an auto-reply? How do I find the "Email notification" label?

View the events of your ticket and look for a recent update from an agent. Look for the Email notification label followed by an ID number in the audit trail. Click the ID next to a notification to view the email notification. If the Email notification event does not exist in the event log, Zendesk did not send an email notification. Ask the end user or customer to check their spam or junk folders.

What is the requirement for an auto-reply or notification rule to reach a customer? How do I activate triggers to send email notifications to an end user? Can I check if my notification rules contain the correct placeholder?

To ensure Zendesk sends email notifications to an end user or customer, follow these steps:

  1. Activate your triggers or notification rules. Standard support triggers that begin with Notify requester of... must be active to reach an end user.
  2. Check your custom conditions. If you customize triggers, ensure that any trigger has conditions that tickets meet.
  3. Verify placeholders. Ensure that your triggers contain the correct placeholder to pull in ticket data.

 

Can I see email delivery failures in the "Agent Workspace"? How do I see why an auto-reply fails in my inbox or dashboard? Can I view reasons for email failure from a comment?

Yes. If you activate the Zendesk Agent Workspace, inbox, or dashboard, you can view reasons for email delivery failure from the comment.
